article_meta.title: ‘President Trump,” reports NPR, “says he hasn’t decided whether he’ll launch a military strike on Iran.” This is a remar

reasoning: No attribution violations and two solid author-prose bias examples identified, but one flagged item is misclassified (clearly stated opinion labeled as Opinion-as-Fact), and several clear biased phrasings in the author’s prose were missed.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: All flagged passages are from the author’s own prose; no quoted or attributed source language was flagged.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: One of three items is not a genuine bias pattern as flagged: the author explicitly marks it as opinion, so it isn’t ‘Opinion Statements Presented as Fact.’
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Missed multiple strong instances of biased prose such as ‘To rob me of that opportunity is a sin,’ ‘timorously acquiesce… its own impotence,’ ‘slow-motion disaster,’ and ‘the most sacred decision… one man’s mood.’
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Two labels are defensible (Sensationalism/Emotionalism; Subjective Qualifying Adjectives). The third mislabels a clearly signposted opinion as ‘Opinion Statements Presented as Fact.’
